explicit_text: My name is Bart Wayne Conner, born on March 28, 1958, in Chicago, Illinois. I grew up with a passion for gymnastics, sparked at age ten when my PE teacher in Chicago noticed my tumbling skills and introduced me to the sport. Today, I run the Bart Conner Gymnastic Academy in Norman, Oklahoma, where I work alongside my former coach and business partner Paul Ziert. Gymnastics has defined my life, and I feel fortunate for the opportunities and friendships it has brought me.

explicit_text: I was born in the Chicago area, specifically at Edgewater Hospital near Evanston, Illinois, just north of Chicago. Growing up in the Chicago suburbs, my brothers and I were very active in sports, playing football, hockey, and baseball. Chicago was home for my family, and my older brother Bruce is still based there as a United Airlines captain. The city and its sports culture played a big role in shaping my early experiences.

explicit_text: I currently live and work in Norman, Oklahoma, where my gymnastics academy is located. This is also where my father retired and lives just a few miles from me. explicit_text: The Bart Conner Gymnastic Academy is where I run my gymnastics business in Norman, Oklahoma. It’s located on a street named Bart Conner Drive, an honor that came about about fifteen years ago thanks to our director Ben Fox appealing to the city. The academy is a thriving place where we train many young gymnasts, and it’s also where I share an office with my former coach and business partner Paul Ziert. This academy represents the heart of my work and passion for gymnastics.

explicit_text: My father went to the University of Kansas after high school before moving to California for work. He studied structural engineering there, which laid the foundation for his career designing airplanes and working with materials. The University of Kansas was an important step in his professional development.

explicit_text: After his time in California, my father moved back to the Chicago area to work for the Portland Cement Association. explicit_text: I attended the University of Oklahoma for four years, where I trained in gymnastics and met my former coach, Paul Ziert. Norman, Oklahoma, where OU is located, has been home to me and my family for many years.

explicit_text: My older brother Bruce has been a captain with United Airlines for the last thirty years. He flies a 747-400 all around the world and is based in Chicago, where we grew up. United Airlines has been a big part of his professional life and his career as a pilot.

explicit_text: My brother Bruce is the World Master Speed Skating Champion, a title he has held while continuing his career as an airline captain. He has qualified for the last three Olympics and continues to compete at a high level despite being in his fifties. His athletic achievements in speed skating are remarkable and inspiring.

explicit_text: My dad introduced me to the story of Baron Pierre de Coubertin when I was in eighth grade, encouraging me to write a term paper about him. Coubertin founded the modern Olympic Games to promote peace, acceptance, and sportsmanship worldwide. Learning about his vision early on helped shape my understanding of sports and life.

explicit_text: The modern Olympic Games have been a guiding influence in my life, teaching lessons about fair play, tolerance, and global friendship.
